briefs.title: Responsible-AI tool / resource for a practice or education setting
briefs.generic_brief: Design and evaluate an AI-assisted tool, activity or resource that supports responsible/ethical AI use or learning for a defined user group. Student refines users and task. The tool is shaped by user research with a defined professional user group (e.g. interviews/questionnaires) and evaluated with them.
worked_examples: A reflection-journal tool with guided prompts supporting responsible, ethical use of generative AI by cybersecurity students.
A prototype web app using AI agents to help lecturers create undergraduate assessments and project materials, informed by user interviews.
Interactive learning activities supporting responsible-AI assessment in law-enforcement contexts.

briefs.title: AI-powered visual / design assistant tool
briefs.generic_brief: Build and evaluate an AI tool that generates or analyses visual content for a user task (interior design, virtual try-on, data visualisation). Student refines the domain.
worked_examples: An AI-powered interior-design stylist that reads room structure to generate realistic redesigns.
A web-based AI virtual changing-room letting users preview clothing on themselves.
A web-based tool for interactive analysis and visualisation of international student-survey data.

briefs.title: AI decision-support / explainable analysis tool for a user group
briefs.generic_brief: Build and evaluate an AI decision-support or explainable-analysis tool for a defined user group or behaviour. Student refines users, data and explainability approach.
worked_examples: A personal health-tracking app analysing wearable physiological metrics to give individualised insights beyond simple thresholds.
A context-aware AI adoption toolkit giving small businesses personalised, conversational decision support.
An explainable-AI model interpreting driving behaviour under varying environmental conditions.

briefs.title: Machine-learning prediction model for a clinical condition
briefs.generic_brief: Build and compare ML/DL models predicting or detecting a clinical condition (e.g. Alzheimer's, Parkinson's) on a real dataset, with explainability. Student refines condition and model family. Models may fuse imaging and tabular clinical data (a multimodal approach) and compare post-hoc explainability techniques.
worked_examples: A comparison of explainable-AI methods for detecting Parkinson's disease from voice biomarkers, prioritising clinician-interpretable decisions.
An evaluation of deep-learning (MLP, TabNet) versus classical ML models for Alzheimer's prediction on tabular and longitudinal clinical data.
A comparative analysis of machine-learning algorithms for Alzheimer's disease prediction.

briefs.title: Machine-learning detection of misinformation / fake news
briefs.generic_brief: Build and evaluate ML (incl. multimodal) models that detect misinformation or fake news against a labelled dataset. Student refines modality and model family.
worked_examples: Fake-news detection using text mining and machine learning.
A comparison of machine-learning models for fake-news detection on the LIAR dataset.
A hybrid multimodal system detecting fake news from combined text and image content.

briefs.title: Machine-learning anti-money-laundering detection
briefs.generic_brief: Compare ML models for detecting money-laundering / illicit transactions in (crypto)financial transaction data. Student refines dataset and model family.
worked_examples: A comparison of machine-learning models for detecting money-laundering in bitcoin transactions using the Elliptic dataset.
A machine-learning approach to detecting money-laundering in blockchain transactions using the Elliptic++ dataset.
An evaluation of alternative machine-learning algorithms for detecting illicit bitcoin transactions on the Elliptic++ dataset.

briefs.title: Physics-guided machine learning for non-destructive materials evaluation
briefs.generic_brief: Apply physics-guided / signal features and ML to detection and characterisation on spatio-temporal sensor data (e.g. thermal NDE of CFRP; vibration; sensor arrays), comparing classical analysis against ML. Student refines material/dataset and method.
worked_examples: Physics-guided machine learning on infrared thermography for detecting subsurface defects in carbon-fibre composites.
A comparison of physics-guided features and machine learning for defect detection and depth estimation in CFRP using eddy-current pulsed thermography.
Physics-driven and machine-learning thermographic signal reconstruction for detecting defects in metallic and composite components.

briefs.title: Machine-learning prediction of molecular / drug toxicity
briefs.generic_brief: Use ML (incl. graph neural networks, fingerprints, tree ensembles) to predict drug-induced toxicity from molecular data, with explainability and optionally a web tool. Student refines target and representation.
worked_examples: A comparison of molecular-fingerprint representations with a stacking classifier to predict drug-induced kidney toxicity.
A web-interface tool that predicts drug-induced kidney toxicity from compound data to support early screening.
An explainable gradient-boosted tree-ensemble model for predicting drug-induced renal toxicity.

briefs.title: Explainable AI for remote-sensing / multi-band image analysis
briefs.generic_brief: Apply explainable AI to remote-sensing or multi-band imagery (space debris, VIS-SWIR-LWIR fusion) for a classification task. Student refines the imagery and task.
worked_examples: A machine-learning classifier for space-debris material and orbital characteristics from multi-modal sensor data, addressing the sim-to-real gap.
A benchmark and fusion evaluation of tri-band visible/SWIR/LWIR imagery for mis-registration-robust vehicle and vessel detection with YOLOv8.
An explainable-AI model for classifying space debris to support orbital-collision risk assessment.

briefs.title: Explainable deep-learning classification of medical images
briefs.generic_brief: Train and evaluate deep-learning models to classify a medical imaging modality (MRI/CT/X-ray/ultrasound/fundus) for a diagnostic task, with explainability (XAI) analysis. Student refines modality and condition.
worked_examples: A deep-learning model with explainable-AI analysis for medical-image classification.
An explainable deep-learning model for multi-class brain-tumour classification from MRI.
A deep-learning model for detecting and classifying bone fractures from X-ray images.

briefs.title: Comparative ML prediction of an environmental or quality metric
briefs.generic_brief: Train and compare ML models to predict an environmental or quality metric (water/air quality, health, sport) from a real public dataset, on agreed metrics. Student refines domain, dataset and models.
worked_examples: A benchmark of four machine-learning regression models for predicting water quality from UK Environment Agency monitoring data.
A machine-learning system predicting PM2.5 air-quality levels from environmental and weather data.
A comparison of LTE versus GSM connectivity for a low-cost IoT air-quality monitoring system.

briefs.title: Deep-learning indoor localisation / motion analysis from sensor signals
briefs.generic_brief: Use IMU / Wi-Fi / wearable signals with ML to localise or analyse motion (indoor positioning, dead-reckoning, gait), evaluated for accuracy. Student refines signal source and method.
worked_examples: Privacy-preserving Wi-Fi-based human activity recognition using RSSI/CSI signals for human-machine interaction.
An investigation of error-reduction and bias-mitigation methods for smartphone pedestrian dead-reckoning using IMU sensor fusion.
A transformer-based deep-learning approach to indoor pedestrian localisation from smartphone IMU data.

briefs.title: Retrieval-augmented AI chatbot / knowledge assistant for a domain
briefs.generic_brief: Build a RAG-based chatbot or knowledge assistant over a defined document corpus (support, charity, university, cyber-intel), evaluated for answer quality. Student refines the corpus.
worked_examples: A retrieval-augmented-generation platform letting users build deployable document-based AI chatbots using their own LLM API credentials.
A retrieval-augmented-generation system over cybersecurity knowledge bases (MITRE ATT&CK, CVEs) to support red-team analysis.
An NLP chatbot to improve how charities communicate with beneficiaries, volunteers and donors.

briefs.title: Intelligent voice / personal assistant and its risks
briefs.generic_brief: Design or evaluate a voice-driven intelligent assistant for a user group, or investigate the security/usability of consumer voice assistants. Student refines build vs investigation.
worked_examples: An investigation into how virtual voice assistants could be improved to support executive functioning and safer online behaviour.
A prototype AI personal assistant that summarises digital messages and suggests actions, with optional voice input.
A study of the security and privacy risks of AI voice assistants and how users perceive their data handling.

briefs.title: Autonomous robot with perception and onboard intelligence
briefs.generic_brief: Develop and evaluate an autonomous robot (ROS-based) for inspection or security, integrating perception (RGB-D/vision) and onboard decision-making, with attention to its data security. Student refines platform and task.
worked_examples: An autonomous tracked security-patrol robot using RGB-D vision and AI threat detection for continuous surveillance.
A study of data-storage and network-security measures for a ROS2 road-inspection robot handling critical-infrastructure data.
An autonomous road-inspection robot built in ROS2 and tested in Gazebo simulation to detect road hazards such as potholes.

briefs.title: Multi-agent simulation / reinforcement learning for resource allocation
briefs.generic_brief: Build a multi-agent simulation or RL approach to a resource-allocation or scalability problem (edge networks, cooperative-competitive systems). Student refines the system.
worked_examples: A reinforcement-learning approach to latency-aware resource allocation in mobile edge networks.
A reinforcement-learning system for adaptive, automated intrusion response in simulated network environments.
A configurable multi-agent simulation framework for studying how reinforcement learning scales in cooperative–competitive settings.

briefs.title: AI classification of physiological signals for mental-health screening
briefs.generic_brief: Use ML on physiological signals (EEG) or build an AI tool for early detection/support of mental-health conditions. Student refines signal and condition.
worked_examples: A cognitive AI tool to support screening of psychological difficulties in late childhood, contextualised for Saudi Arabia.
An AI model classifying emotion from EEG signals for early detection of depression and anxiety.

briefs.title: Simulation & performance evaluation of a modern network architecture
briefs.generic_brief: Using a discrete-event network simulator, model a modern network architecture (e.g. Open RAN, SDN, a 5G/6G RAN split) against a conventional baseline, then measure and compare performance under controlled traffic and channel conditions. The student selects the architecture, simulator scenarios and metrics during refinement.
worked_examples: An NS-3 model of Open RAN (CU/DU/RU split) versus a monolithic RAN, compared on latency, throughput and packet-delivery ratio under increasing load.
An SDN vs traditional-routing comparison under link-failure and congestion scenarios in Mininet.
A 5G urban-microcell propagation study comparing scheduler behaviours under varying user density.

briefs.title: ML intrusion / anomaly detection for networks and connected systems
briefs.generic_brief: Train and evaluate ML models to detect intrusions or anomalies in network/IoT traffic against a labelled dataset, comparing detection performance and false-positive behaviour. Student refines threat type, dataset and model family. The detection layer may combine a rule/signature approach with an ML layer (a hybrid comparison), and the same approach extends to spotting tampered or spoofed readings from connected devices.
worked_examples: A GAN-augmented intrusion-detection model that synthesises minority-class traffic to handle class imbalance in IoT network datasets.
A GRU-based deep-learning intrusion-detection system for temporal anomaly detection in IoT smart-device network traffic.
An explainable-AI anomaly-detection approach for IoT smart-device networks, comparing supervised and autoencoder-based methods.

briefs.title: ML detection of insider threats
briefs.generic_brief: Build and evaluate ML models that detect malicious insider activity from system logs or user-behaviour graphs, addressing class imbalance and explainability. Student refines data source and model.
worked_examples: Unsupervised graph-based anomaly detection for identifying insider threats.
A hybrid graph-neural-network model with adversarial sampling for insider-threat detection.
Machine-learning detection of insider threats from system-activity logs.

briefs.title: Digital twin for cyber-attack simulation / detection
briefs.generic_brief: Build a digital-twin / virtual model of a network and use it to simulate attacks or detect novel anomalies (e.g. in 5G core). Student refines the modelled environment and method.
worked_examples: An AI-driven digital twin that simulates cyber attacks against a modelled network environment.
A digital-twin framework for detecting novel anomalies in 5G core networks.
A machine-learning intrusion-detection system embedded in a digital twin of network traffic.

briefs.title: Security and analysis of large language models
briefs.generic_brief: Investigate LLM security — fingerprinting, prompt-injection defence, attack-surface or output-reconstruction analysis — with implementation and evaluation. Student refines the specific question.
worked_examples: A web-application firewall using local-LLM inspection to detect obfuscated malicious HTTP requests that evade signature-based rules.
A defensive approach combining multiple LLMs to detect and prevent prompt-injection attacks on LLM-powered systems.
Fingerprinting large language models through analysis of their network traffic.

briefs.title: Blockchain trust / resilience investigation
briefs.generic_brief: Investigate blockchain's resilience or trust properties (e.g. MITM resistance, provenance/anti-counterfeiting) against an alternative, with implementation and evaluation. Student refines the use case.
worked_examples: A comparative analysis of blockchain versus PKI resilience against man-in-the-middle attacks on secure communications.
An investigation into using blockchain to authenticate trainers and prevent counterfeit e-commerce sales.

briefs.title: Security auditing / vulnerability-analysis tooling
briefs.generic_brief: Design and evaluate a tool that audits or statically analyses an application/platform for security weaknesses (e.g. secrets, misconfig, version diffing) and reports actionable findings. Student refines the target.
worked_examples: A command-line security tool performing static analysis and version-diffing of APK files to surface hardcoded secrets and risky changes.
A security auditing, remediation and validation framework for a popular low-code website-building platform.

briefs.title: Interactive digital-forensics / cybercrime investigation simulator
briefs.generic_brief: Design an interactive simulation or tool teaching or supporting digital-forensic / cybercrime investigation, evaluated for educational or investigative value. Student refines scenario.
worked_examples: An interactive digital-forensics training simulation built in Unity for developing cybercrime-investigation skills.
A vision-language-model tool to help police digital-forensics units triage the backlog of seized-device data.

briefs.title: Ultra-wideband indoor tracking / access system
briefs.generic_brief: Develop and evaluate a UWB indoor tracking, asset-location or proximity-access system, measuring performance. Student refines the build.
worked_examples: A UWB real-time-location framework for proximity-based workstation access control, handling signal jitter and sleep/wake recovery.
A software tool that visualises Ultra-Wideband indoor asset-tracking data on a live 3D map.

briefs.title: Human-factors study of security / compliance behaviour
briefs.generic_brief: Investigate human or contextual factors affecting security or compliance behaviour (e.g. age-verification compliance). The findings must produce a defined output — security/compliance design requirements, a behavioural framework situated in the literature, or a prototype intervention evaluated with users. Student refines the behaviour. [SCREENING: a behavioural study alone does not meet the UG bar.]
worked_examples: An investigation of the implementation and contextual factors affecting user compliance with online age-verification measures.

briefs.title: Machine-learning detection of phishing / spam under adversarial conditions
briefs.generic_brief: Build and evaluate ML detection of phishing or spam (emails/websites), including robustness to adversarial or AI-generated content. Student refines target and embeddings/models.
worked_examples: A comparative study of embeddings and ML/DL models for phishing email and URL detection, and their robustness to adversarial text attacks.
A secure local mail system with machine-learning-based spam detection evaluated under adversarial conditions.
An adaptive transfer-learning model for robustly detecting phishing websites despite evasion tactics and unseen patterns.

briefs.title: Effectiveness of cybersecurity awareness training (human factors)
briefs.generic_brief: Study how cybersecurity awareness or knowledge transfer works in organisations or social networks, measuring effectiveness/fidelity, then turn the findings into a defined output — training/awareness design requirements, a framework situated in the literature, or an awareness intervention or tool evaluated with users. Student refines population and intervention. [SCREENING: a measurement study alone does not meet the UG bar.]
worked_examples: A longitudinal qualitative study of how social interaction shapes people's understanding of SIM-swapping fraud.
A comparison of video versus gamified cybersecurity-awareness methods for learning and communicating security advice.
A study of how accurately cybersecurity advice is transmitted through informal social networks, and how to improve its fidelity.

briefs.title: IoT sensor network / protocol prototype and evaluation
briefs.generic_brief: Build and evaluate an IoT sensor-network prototype (ESP32/multi-hop/protocol comparison) for a monitoring application, measuring transmission performance. Student refines sensors and protocols.
worked_examples: A comparison of IoT transport protocols (HTTP, MQTT, CoAP) and encryption levels on a temperature-sensor prototype.
A comparison of path-planning algorithms (Dijkstra, A*, Theta*) for routing offshore wind-farm subsea cables.
The design and evaluation of a multi-hop wireless sensor network built with ESP32 microcontrollers, comparing routing protocols.

briefs.title: Cloud-native / IoT platform for remote environmental monitoring
briefs.generic_brief: Build a cloud-native or IoT platform for remote monitoring (precision agriculture, isolated areas), evaluated for reliability. Student refines the domain.
worked_examples: A cloud-native platform processing Sentinel-2 satellite imagery to monitor and classify crop health, with a connectivity-tolerant dashboard.
A novel-writing application with a scene-based organisation system that auto-composes manuscripts to standard file formats.
A cloud-native crop-monitoring platform using satellite data to support precision-agriculture decisions in low-connectivity areas.

briefs.title: Forensic text mining of digital evidence corpora
briefs.generic_brief: Apply text mining / NLP to a corpus of digital evidence (emails, logs) for forensic intelligence and pattern/anomaly discovery. Student refines corpus and method.
worked_examples: NLP-based linguistic anomaly detection on the Enron email corpus to flag suspicious communications for forensic analysts.
Text mining and pattern recognition over digital communications such as the Enron corpus to surface forensic evidence and hidden relationships.

briefs.title: Research-oriented software tool benchmarked against an incumbent
briefs.generic_brief: Design and build a working software tool that addresses a specific gap in existing tooling, benchmark it quantitatively against the established alternative, and run a usability evaluation with target users. Student selects the tool domain and the benchmark dimensions in refinement.
worked_examples: A research-oriented version-control system plus GUI, benchmarked against Git on large-file handling and evaluated for usability with researchers.
A lightweight data-pipeline tool benchmarked against an established framework on throughput and ease of use.
A domain-specific code editor extension evaluated against the default workflow.

briefs.title: Staff scheduling / rostering application for an organisation
briefs.generic_brief: Analyse a shift-scheduling workflow and build an application (optionally with optimisation) that improves rota creation, evaluated against the manual baseline. Student refines the sector.
worked_examples: An application that automatically generates staff rotas for shift-based workplaces.
A digital staff-scheduling tool for small businesses, replacing manual paper or spreadsheet rotas.
A scheduling application supporting middle management to reduce workload and staffing errors.

briefs.title: E-commerce / stock-management application
briefs.generic_brief: Design and build an e-commerce, marketplace or stock-management application for an organisation or user group, evaluated for usability/efficiency. Student refines the domain.
worked_examples: An e-commerce automation system for an online retail workflow.
A student-focused e-commerce marketplace for buying and selling second-hand goods on campus.
A user-centred stock-management system for micro and small enterprises in Southeast Asia.

briefs.title: Conceptual modelling / novel information-system design
briefs.generic_brief: Design a conceptual modelling language or novel information system for an unconventional domain. Student refines the domain.
worked_examples: The design of a conceptual modelling language extending UML/SysML to represent animals as stakeholders in interspecies information systems.

briefs.title: Secondary-data analysis of adoption / satisfaction in a population
briefs.generic_brief: Use existing public datasets (plus light qualitative sources) to analyse barriers, motivators or satisfaction in a population (e.g. hearing-aid adoption). Student refines the domain. [SCREENING: analysis must feed a defined output — design requirements, a literature-situated framework, or a prototype — to meet the UG bar.]
worked_examples: A study of the barriers and motivators affecting hearing-aid satisfaction and use among adults aged 50 and over.
An analysis of hearing-aid satisfaction among elderly users using secondary data from the English Longitudinal Study of Ageing.
A quantitative analysis of barriers and motivators to hearing-aid adoption among older adults using public secondary data.

briefs.title: Comparative time-series forecasting with statistical, ML and deep-learning models
briefs.generic_brief: Compare statistical, ML and deep-learning (incl. foundation) models for forecasting a real time series (finance, hydrology, energy, deforestation, network), with uncertainty/accuracy evaluation. Student refines domain and models.
worked_examples: An AI framework using behavioural analysis to detect ransomware early, before large-scale encryption, against evasion techniques such as polymorphism.
A comparison of foundation time-series models (Google TimesFM) against ARIMA, Prophet and LSTM for forecasting Brent crude oil prices.
Time-series anomaly detection and forecasting of tropical deforestation in Borneo from Sentinel-2 satellite imagery.

briefs.title: Spatial and statistical analysis of public crime / socio-economic data
briefs.generic_brief: Analyse and visualise patterns in public crime or socio-economic datasets (theft, shoplifting, house prices, business survival), addressing bias/fairness where relevant, and deliver a defined output beyond the analysis — an interactive tool or dashboard, a set of decision/design requirements, or a fairness framework situated in the literature. Student refines dataset and question. [SCREENING: analysis and visualisation alone do not meet the UG bar.]
worked_examples: A data analysis and visualisation of factors influencing shoplifting in the Northumbria Police area using open crime data.
A data-engineering and visualisation pipeline analysing spatial and temporal patterns of theft-from-the-person across ten London boroughs.
An analysis of the determinants of five-year UK business survival using ONS Business Demography open data.

briefs.title: Business-intelligence analytics dashboard for decision support
briefs.generic_brief: Design and build a BI/analytics dashboard summarising and forecasting from a real organisational dataset to support decisions. Student refines the domain and metrics.
worked_examples: An AI-driven market-analytics dashboard that summarises unstructured business text and forecasts emerging trends.
An AI assistant that auto-generates authentic onboarding learning experiences from a company's real documents and workflows.
A business-intelligence dashboard turning banking transactional data into sales-performance insights using low-code BI tools.

briefs.title: Robotic Process Automation of a repetitive business workflow
briefs.generic_brief: Build and test an RPA prototype automating an end-to-end rule-based business workflow (rentals, screening), against the manual baseline. Student refines the workflow.
worked_examples: A robotic-process-automation workflow automating rental-application screening and viewing scheduling across email, spreadsheets and calendars.
A robotic-process-automation prototype automating the repetitive administrative workflow of car rental.

briefs.title: Mixed-methods study comparing a local survey against national open data
briefs.generic_brief: Design a primary survey aligned to questions in a national open dataset, collect and analyse local responses quantitatively and qualitatively, and compare local findings against national trends on a chosen behaviour or attitude question, then translate the findings into a defined output — a set of evidence-based design requirements, or a framework interpreted against the literature. NOTE: original recruited via a hospital (ethics/access lock); portable variant uses ordinary public recruitment. [SCREENING: primary collection required, and the project must deliver design requirements, a literature-situated framework, or a participant-evaluated prototype — collection and analysis alone do not meet the UG bar.]
worked_examples: A study of barriers and motivators for hearing-aid use in adults 50+, comparing a local survey against national datasets (ELSA / EuroTrak).
A study of public attitudes to data privacy comparing a local survey against a national open survey series.
A comparison of local commuting behaviour against national transport open data.

briefs.title: Student finance / spend-tracking tool
briefs.generic_brief: Design, prototype and user-evaluate a budgeting or spend-tracking tool addressing students' money-management needs, using a UX/HCI method. Student refines features and platform.
worked_examples: A rapid-entry spending-tracker app designed for university students' budgeting needs.
An evaluation of the usability and effectiveness of student spending-tracking applications.
A simplified, low-complexity spend-tracking app designed and evaluated with students.

briefs.title: Student–lecturer communication tool
briefs.generic_brief: Design and evaluate an alternative to email for structured student–lecturer communication, addressing etiquette anxiety and inconsistent responses. Student refines the channel and evaluation.
worked_examples: A study and prototype exploring alternatives to email for student–lecturer communication, addressing student anxiety about contacting tutors.
A UX prototype redesigning student–lecturer communication to reduce the ambiguity and anxiety of email etiquette.
A designed-and-evaluated structured alternative to email for student–lecturer communication.

briefs.title: Digital wellbeing / self-regulation intervention
briefs.generic_brief: Design and evaluate a digital intervention supporting wellbeing, anxiety or self-regulation for a defined group, informed by research. Student refines the population and intervention.
worked_examples: A digital intervention addressing student anxiety during the transition to higher education.
A mobile study app using self-regulation and environmental-restructuring features to reduce academic procrastination.
A mobile application supporting menopausal women and their employers in the workplace through education and coping tools.

briefs.title: Student learning-support / accessibility tool
briefs.generic_brief: Design and evaluate a tool supporting learning, study management or accessibility for students (incl. neurodivergent or disabled users). Student refines the need and platform.
worked_examples: A digital intervention addressing sensory barriers for neurodivergent students transitioning to university.
A web-based learner issue-tracker for surfacing and managing shared student learning difficulties.
An accessibility app helping people with epilepsy engage safely with heritage sites.

briefs.title: Clinical / health management or monitoring application
briefs.generic_brief: Design and build a management or monitoring application supporting a clinical workflow, condition or health-student placement, evaluated with users or data. Student refines the setting.
worked_examples: A web-based clinical management system for remote monitoring of Parkinson's patients between infrequent clinic visits.
A prototype mobile app supporting allied-health students through clinical placements.
A clinical management system preparing health and life-science students for clinical placements.

briefs.title: Human perception of misinformation (eye-tracking / interface study)
briefs.generic_brief: Study how people perceive misinformation using eye-tracking or interface experiments. The attention patterns identified must feed a defined output — interface design requirements, a credibility/attention framework situated in the literature, or a redesigned interface element evaluated with participants. Student refines the stimulus and measure. [SCREENING: an attention study alone does not meet the UG bar — findings must inform design requirements, a literature-situated framework, or a participant-evaluated prototype.]
worked_examples: An eye-tracking study of how people visually attend to misinformative political imagery compared with accurate news.
An eye-tracking investigation of why readers are vulnerable to political misinformation, identifying attention patterns and credibility cues.
An eye-tracking study optimising web-interface design to reduce susceptibility to political misinformation.

briefs.title: Study-skill micro-practice tool
briefs.generic_brief: Design and evaluate a micro-practice / pattern-recognition tool helping students learn a study skill (e.g. referencing). Student refines the skill.
worked_examples: A micro-practice tool using pattern recognition to help students learn Harvard referencing rather than relying on generators.

briefs.title: Web application for personal tracking and productivity
briefs.generic_brief: Design and build a web application for tracking a personal domain (sleep, time, reading, productivity) with data capture and analysis, evaluated with users or against an alternative build. Student refines domain.
worked_examples: A fault-tolerant message-queue system for real-time web applications, testing retry mechanisms and AI-based failure prediction under simulated failures.
A full-stack medical-diagnosis web application delivering AI disease-prediction through interfaces designed for both patients and clinicians.
A web application that automatically tracks time on work activities by capturing data from calendar tools.

briefs.title: Behavioural / HCI study of social-media use and recommender effects
briefs.generic_brief: Investigate how users experience a social-media platform or recommender mechanism (TikTok, algorithmic bubbles) using behavioural, ethnographic or simulation methods, then convert the insight into a defined output — design requirements or solutions, a framework discussed against the literature, or a prototype intervention taken back to users. Student refines platform and method. [SCREENING: an experience study alone does not meet the UG bar — it must deliver design requirements, a literature-situated framework, or a participant-evaluated prototype.]
worked_examples: A study of the cybersecurity and privacy risks young people face using TikTok for informal learning, with proposed design solutions.
An investigation into whether K-means clustering stays stable as social-media users' behaviour and preferences shift over time.
A study and tool addressing the spread of misinformation on TikTok when the platform is used for informal education.

briefs.title: Interactive companion / utility application for a user activity
briefs.generic_brief: Design and evaluate an interactive companion or utility app supporting a user activity (travel, music practice) with real-time feedback. Student refines the activity.
worked_examples: An interactive travel-companion app letting families attach personal memories (photos, video, text) to points of interest.
An interactive travel-companion app for exploring Newcastle points of interest and attaching personal memories to them.
An interactive drumming-tutor app using microphone input and onset detection to give real-time feedback on timing and accuracy.

briefs.title: Gesture / technology control of stage lighting
briefs.generic_brief: Build and evaluate a system controlling stage lighting via modern interaction (body gestures, poses, devices). Student refines the interaction method.
worked_examples: A gesture- and pose-controlled system for operating stage lighting without specialist lighting desks.
A mobile web application letting performers and audiences control venue stage-lighting colour, brightness and direction.

briefs.title: Games design study or playable prototype investigating a player-experience question
briefs.generic_brief: Build or study a game (or game feature) to investigate a player-experience question such as difficulty, immersion, engagement or skill, with playtesting/evaluation. Student refines the question and engine.
worked_examples: An experimental study of how video-game sound affects concentration during academic tasks, with digital data collection and statistical analysis.
An investigation into what separates fair, rewarding game difficulty from frustrating 'bad' difficulty and its effect on player engagement.
A game prototype exploring how boss-encounter difficulty can be raised through enemy mechanics and behaviour rather than stat scaling.

briefs.title: VR simulation for training or heritage recreation
briefs.generic_brief: Design and user-evaluate a VR simulation for training (driving, safety) or heritage recreation, as a proof-of-concept. Student refines scenario and hardware.
worked_examples: A VR training simulator (with an industry partner) teaching lorry drivers safe trailer decoupling and yard procedures.
A VR training simulator replicating HGV coupling/uncoupling procedures to cut the cost and risk of real-world driver training.
A user-centred VR simulation prototype (Unreal Engine 5) for training apprentice dental nurses, evaluated for usability and satisfaction.
